prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial unfolds as a rapid montage combining live-action, stylized imagery, title cards, and video-game footage.

It opens with a dark, blurred image dominated by red and black tones, with a small multicolored “N” logo visible near the lower right. This cuts to a very bright, washed-out shot in what appears to be a crowded setting; a pale rounded object or head is centered, but the details are unclear.

A green-tinted close-up follows, showing several people in a crowded environment. A young woman leans toward the camera while another person sits or stands behind her. The lighting is artificial and club-like. The sequence then cuts to a title card reading “TUROK” with smaller text underneath that appears to say “DINOSAUR HUNTER,” set against a pale pink and white textured background. The title flashes or changes brightness in the following frame.

The next shots continue the intense green visual style: a close-up of part of a face and hair fills the right side of the frame while shadowy figures appear on the left. This is followed by an aerial view of a city intersection with cars and streets seen from above. The montage then cuts into first-person video-game footage with an orange-tinted sky or landscape, a weapon visible at the bottom right, and a small HUD element at the bottom left. A bright white-pink transition washes the frame.

A blue-lit close-up shows a face or mask on the left side of the screen, with a pale raised hand and blurred figures in the background. This transitions to first-person shooter gameplay in a gray interior corridor: a gun is visible in the foreground, with human figures ahead. A title card then appears reading “GOLDENEYE 007” on a greenish textured background. More gameplay follows in a four-panel split-screen layout, showing first-person views and weapon elements. Another first-person game view appears outdoors or on a bridge/road, with a weapon visible and a dark landscape ahead.

The commercial then shifts to a bright live-action corridor or transit-like interior. Uniformed or formally dressed people walk through the space, while yellow/orange costumed or brightly dressed figures move in the background. This is followed by very overexposed close-up shots where yellow/orange and blue-white shapes are visible but not clearly identifiable.

A gold title card appears reading “STAR WARS SHADOWS OF THE EMPIRE.” After that, game footage shows a vehicle or craft moving through a pale desert- or snow-like environment toward a large blocky structure. Another game shot shows a humanoid or robotic enemy in a tiled interior, with HUD labels including “Health” and “Laser.”

The next section uses stark white lighting. A line or group of people in white clothing is shown against a nearly white background. Then a person dressed in bright red appears among many white-clad figures. A wider shot shows a large crowd of people arranged across the frame, most in white, with one or more red-clad figures standing out near the center.

Another title card appears with the text “EXTREME-G” on a pale blue-white background. It cuts to a red/orange glowing title or graphic, but the text is too overexposed to read clearly. A pale blue-white flash follows. Then racing-game footage appears: a futuristic vehicle rides along a bright orange/yellow track, with green scenery and HUD elements including a position marker, a timer, and speed/indicator graphics.

Near the end, the commercial cuts to a dark nighttime city shot looking up at a building with lit windows and streetlights. The final frames show a black background with white text reading “get N or get out.” The “N” is a multicolored Nintendo 64-style logo. Below it is the website “www.nintendo.com.” The last frame centers the multicolored “N” logo with thin horizontal lines extending from it and the same website address at the bottom.
